name: Microsoft Word App
description: Rules for Microsoft Word document writing and editing workflows, including new documents, revisions, formatting, saving, and exporting.
whenToApply: The phase mainly happens in Microsoft Word or a DOCX document.
The phase needs to create, edit, revise, structure, format, save, or export a Word document.
whenNotToApply: Word is only a final viewer and the editing happens in another app.
The phase only reads a DOCX and answers in chat without modifying or generating a document.
generationGuidance: Confirm whether the agent is creating a new document, editing an existing document, or saving a copy.
Separate source material, user requirements, agent assumptions, and content that needs confirmation.
Require user approval before overwriting, deleting, or heavily rewriting existing user content.
After saving or exporting, validate visible document content plus file name, format, and location.
hardRequirements: Do not treat generated chat text as a completed Word file.
Every save or export phase must validate the file exists and its visible content or structure matches the target.
Missing source material or conflicting writing requirements must become a stop condition.

runtimeReference.title: Microsoft Word App Rules
runtimeReference.purpose: Word-specific writing, editing, save/export, and validation rules.
whenToRead: Before executing any phase that creates, edits, saves, or exports a Word document.
